name: Beans / Legumes

book_claims.claim: Regular consumption helps prevent accumulation of belly fat and chronic disease
book_claims.mechanism: Fibre-rich food that after gut microbe processing releases protective metabolites; low in empty calories compared to processed foods

book_claims.claim: Part of fibre-rich diet that causes spontaneous weight loss and metabolic improvement
book_claims.mechanism: High fibre induces early satiety; source of plant protein, vitamins, phytocompounds and minerals

supplementary_research.finding: A 2014 meta-analysis (CMAJ, PMID 24871476) of 21 RCTs found that eating one serving of pulses (beans, lentils, chickpeas, peas) per day significantly reduced LDL cholesterol by 5%, supporting cardiovascular risk reduction.
A 2016 meta-analysis (Am J Clin Nutr, PMID 26269228) of 21 RCTs found that pulse consumption significantly increased satiety by 31% compared to control diets, supporting the weight management claims.

The INTERMAP study (Asia Pac J Clin Nutr, PMID 15228991) and a 2004 analysis found that legume consumption was the single most protective dietary factor for longevity across multiple cultures, with an 8% reduction in mortality risk per 20g daily intake.

A 2017 systematic review (Nutrients, PMID 28233404) found that regular bean consumption was associated with reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, obesity, type 2 diabetes, and colorectal cancer, with fiber and resistant starch as primary mechanisms.

culinary_pairings.source: Aromatic base for nearly all bean preparations worldwide
Classic spice pairing across multiple bean-centric cuisines
Acid and umami complement creamy beans; classic in chili and fasolada
Finishing fat that carries flavor and adds healthy monounsaturated fats
Complementary amino acids for complete protein; rice and beans is universal
Foundation aromatic in virtually every bean dish worldwide
Vitamin C improves non-heme iron absorption from beans

seasonality: Dried beans available year-round. Fresh shell beans (cranberry, fava, lima) seasonal in summer. Canned beans are nutritionally equivalent to home-cooked for most purposes.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Mix of soluble and insoluble fiber plus resistant starch. Feeds beneficial gut bacteria producing butyrate and other SCFAs. Significantly more fiber per calorie than any animal protein.
High in lysine, low in methionine/tryptophan. Complementary with grains for complete amino acid profile. Lower TMAO production than animal protein.
Excellent source. Essential for DNA synthesis and methylation. Black beans and kidney beans among the richest sources.
Important for blood pressure regulation. Most adults are deficient; beans are among the best food sources.
Prebiotic fiber that feeds beneficial gut bacteria. Content increases when beans are cooked and cooled.

synergies.description: Part of the core fibre-rich food group; complementary amino acids for complete protein
Vitamin C from citrus dramatically improves non-heme iron absorption from beans
